figures.caption: Figure 1: (a) Schematic illustration of the coupled Dirac cones model for even-layered MBT thin films. The top and bottom surface of each septuple layer (SL) is shown in red and green colors, respectively and correspond to opposite signs of the Dirac kinetic energy term. The arrows denote the four model parameters: JD is the magnetic exchange from the neighboring SL, JS is the intralayer exchange, and ∆S (∆D) is the intralayer/interlayer hybridization. The red and bright blue arrows indicate up and down spin alignments. (b) Heat map of the layer-resolved Chern contributions for different spin alignments in a 6-SL MBT film. (c) Sum of all contributions (i.e., total Chern number Ctot) for the respective spin configurations.

Figure 2: Frequency dependent anomalous Hall conductivity (σxy) for representative 6-SL spin configurations. (b-c) Corresponding low-energy band structures in the vicinity of the Γ point with band gaps Eg=18.9 meV and Eg=91.6 meV for Ctot=1 and Ctot=0, respectively. (d-e) Layer-by-layer contributions to the Chern number for the same configurations.

Figure 3: Topological MO switching in a 6-SL MnBi2Te4 thin film. (a) Schematic of incident light (h̄ω) falling on a spin configuration with parallel magnetization at the outermost SLs, resulting in light transmission (h̄ω′′) and a quantized Faraday rotation angle (θF ). (b) Frequency-dependent Faraday angle for two representative spin configurations: the solid line corresponds to parallel SL alignment (C = 1), exhibiting quantized MO response, while the dashed line corresponds to antiparallel alignment (C = 0) showing a vanishing response. (c) Illustration of the antiparallel configuration, where opposite magnetization at the outermost SLs leads to zero Faraday rotation angle (θF = 0).

Figure 4: (a) Heat map of layer-resolved Chern contributions across different spin configurations considered for a 12-SL MBT thin film. (b) Corresponding total Chern number (Ctot) map, identifying Ctot = 0, 1, and 2 phases. (c-e) Representative layer-resolved Chern contributions for Ctot = 0, 1, and 2 states, respectively. (f-h) Frequency-dependence of the Faraday rotation angle (θF ) for the same three configurations. The Ctot = 2 phase also exhibits quantized MO response with doubled low-frequency θF , consistent with higher Chern number value, relative to Ctot = 1 phase.
